target.name: Zeta Surgical
target.founder: Jose Amich, Raahil Sha, William Gormley

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Zeta Surgical is a Series A biotech company currently valued at $100M post-money (having raised $23M to date). The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Series B-stage comparables such as Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, implies a $200M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $810M (range: $600M–$3.3B). Public-comp anchoring using a 6.6x price-to-sales median across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L could not be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure for the private target.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $170M to $931M, with a median of $505M. The current $100M valuation sits below the low bound, reflecting the typical discount applied to Series A pricing versus later-stage or public comparables. Key uncertainties center on the wide dispersion within the peer set (600M–$3.3B) and the absence of revenue metrics needed for multiples-based validation. Stage-progression and peer anchoring pull in opposite directions, warranting sensitivity to peer selection and stage-specific discount rates.
